stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Twitch Allows Parents To Restrict Teens From Livestreaming 07/22/2026
stories.summary: by , July 21, 2026 For the first time since its initial launch in 2011, Twitch is introducing parental control features, which include the ability for guardians to block their teen’s ability to broadcast livestreams on the platform. In addition to content classification labels and default safety settings applied to teen accounts, Twitch is attempting to allow parents and guardians to have more power over what their teens -- age 13-17 -- are able to do on the platform. Parents can now create an account and link it with their teen’s account, giving them access to account-management features and a weekly email update regarding their teen’s on-platform activity.

stories.title: Fortnite Cube Sprites Release Date Explained
stories.summary: What are the Fortnite Cube Sprites? This is a new feature that we’re expecting to arrive in the next Fortnite update. It’ll be a brand-new variant for each of the sprites. joining Holofoil, Gold, and Galaxy as a new type of collectible. But how exactly will they work? Chapter 7 Season 3 has gone all in on Sprites. A little collectible you can track down in each game. Giving you unique Sprite powers. But also, something to extract and collect, so you can use them in the cosmetics in the Battle Plus. Plus, the bragging rights of just having a really rare sprite. So how do the Fortnite Cube Sprites work and when is their release date going to be? What are Fortnite Cube Sprites?

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Leeds United closing in on next transfer after James Trafford as £75m clause suggested
stories.summary: Leeds United look set to sell Jack Harrison with a big James Trafford release clause claim also made as the Whites eye their next two transfers. Huge ambition have already been shown by Leeds in the summer window with Harry Wilson joining as a free agent and Tarik Muharemovic completing a move from Sassuolo. Former Juventus centre-back Muharemovic has become Leeds' second-most expensive signing after joining in a £34. 1million deal. Free agent Julian Brandt is also being eyed by Daniel Farke's side along with Liverpool star Harvey Elliott.

stories.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
stories.summary: Carlo Ancelotti and Pep Guardiola have both held talks with Italy over the national team coaching job Italy approached Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before holding talks with Pep Guardiola. The Azzurri have been without a head coach since Gennaro Gattuso resigned in April following the country's failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup. Earlier this week, Italian Football Federation (FIGC) president Giovanni Malago said talks had been held with Guardiola, who left Manchester City this summer after 10 years, and "exceptions" could be made for his salary.

stories.title: The Game Recalls Early Encounters With Nipsey Hussle During VERZUZ With YG
stories.summary: The Game got nostalgic during his VERZUZ face-off with YG , recalling long-ago encounters with the late Nipsey Hussle before performing their collaboration “They Roll. ” During the show on Thursday (June 23), the Compton rapper said that his Blood affiliations never got in the way of his friendship with Hussle. “A n***a love for the [C]rips, though,” he said onstage. “RIP Nip. ” He then recalled his first meeting with Hussle. “I pulled up on Crenshaw and Slauson,” he began. “I saw a n***a with a Jheri curl selling incense. That n***a said, ‘I’mma see you. ’” “A year later, I pulled back up on him,” The Game continued. “The n***a had braids and a mixtape. He threw it to me.

stories.title: Julia Rosinitoba turns pain into purpose through dance
stories.summary: For Julia Rosinitoba, dance is more than performance — it is where she has found healing, purpose and the strength to keep moving forward as a single mother of two. The contemporary dancer will take centre stage in the University of the South Pacific's Oceania Centre for Arts, Culture and Pacific Studies (OCACPS) production of Sina, which premieres on August 7-8. The production marks the Centre's first major performance since December 2023 and officially relaunches its performing and musical arts programmes. Originally from Kadavu, with maternal links to Savusavu, Julia's journey began as a young girl following the movements of her church dance group.

stories.title: Ajax and Racing want Jofre Torrents as Barcelona teen set for season-long loan
stories.summary: Ajax and Racing are vying for Barcelona's 19-year-old left-back Jofre Torrents, who could join them on a season-long loan this summer after missing much of last season due to an ankle injury. Hansi Flick is preparing his team with available options at left-back ahead of the upcoming season.

stories.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
stories.summary: “Concert stage atmosphere with vibrant stage lighting, artist holding a national flag on stage in front of a cheering crowd, realistic live performance concert photography style. ” During her massive concert in Bogotá, Spanish singer Rosalía delivered one of the most emotional moments of the night by sending a warm shout-out to a group of Costa Rican fans who traveled all the way to Colombia just to see her perform live. The gesture, which quickly went viral across social media, highlighted the artist’s close connection with her Latin American fanbase and the dedication of concertgoers who cross borders for her live shows. 1.

stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor
stories.summary:
Rudakubana is serving a 52-year sentence after the fatal stabbing of three girls and the attempted murder of eight other children and two adults at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport, Merseyside, in July 2024.

The killer, who was 17 at the time of the attack, pleaded guilty to killing Alice da Silva Aguiar, nine, Bebe King, six, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven.

Rudakabana was transferred from the category A prison to Broadmoor high-security hospital on Thursday in a move that does not change his sentence, the MoJ said.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of beating Arsenal to £100m-rated Bradley Barcola transfer
stories.summary: LIVERPOOL are confident of beating Arsenal to £100million-rated Bradley Barcola. The Gunners are eyeing the Paris St Germain and France ace after missing out to Chelsea on the signing of £117m Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. Bradkey Barcola is in-demand this summer Credit: Getty Morgan Rogers signed for Chelsea in a £117m deal Credit: Getty But SunSport understands Liverpool are in the driving seat for Barcola , who is also wanted by Bayern Munich . The Reds are on the hunt for a marquee forward signing after the departure of veteran Mo Salah . Sign up for the Arsenal newsletter Thank you!

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

stories.title: Death of husband in HSE care leaves unanswered questions
stories.summary: The wife of a man who took his own life while receiving psychiatric treatment in Cork has questioned how he was able to do so while he was being held in a secure mental health unit. 67-year-old Mark Reilly was admitted to Cork University Hospital's Acute Mental Health Unit in March 2022 because he was suicidal. Cork University Hospital and the HSE have apologised for failings in Mark's care. Nancy Reilly said Mark, a former software engineer, community volunteer and teacher, had shown no signs of mental illness during their 37-year marriage before developing what she believes was a psychosis from late 2021.

stories.title: Massimo Bottura Turned A Modena Farmhouse Into One of Italy’s Most Coveted Stays
stories.summary: At Casa Maria Luigia, owners Massimo Bottura and Lara Gilmore welcome guests into their world of food, art, music, and community.

stories.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
stories.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

stories.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
stories.summary: Gruesome images of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez's dismembered body were shown in court on the first day of a preliminary hearing against singer D4vd, whose real name is David Anthony Burke. The evidence, including graphic crime scene photos, links the 21-year-old Burke to the murder and sexual abuse of the teenager, with prosecutors detailing his online purchases of tools associated with the crime. Burke has pleaded not guilty, and the hearing aims to determine if there is enough evidence for the case to proceed to trial.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on 60 trading partners, accounting for the vast majority of its imports, over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India. They come into effect on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

stories.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
stories.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester today, marking a significant step in the government's plan to decentralize power from Westminster. This initiative aims to enhance regional governance, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join the site by the end of next year. The meeting will focus on placing devolution at the forefront of the government's mission to improve economic conditions across the UK.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

stories.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a large-scale effort to steal technology from top U.S. AI models, specifically claiming it "distilled" capabilities from Anthropic's Fable AI for its K3 model. This allegation follows the unveiling of K3, which has drawn global attention for potentially narrowing the gap between Western and Chinese AI technologies. The U.S. government is investigating Moonshot's access to restricted Nvidia servers used in training its models.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A panel of federal health advisers on Thursday narrowly recommended easing access to several peptides popular with wellness influencers and celebrities, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals have not been shown to be safe or effective. In a series of votes, outside experts to the Food and Drug Administration advised the agency to drop current restrictions barring pharmacies from producing injectable peptides with names like BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV. The panel’s vote is not binding but is likely to embolden the health secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr , who has described himself as a “big fan” of peptides and vowed to overturn FDA restrictions put in place under Joe Biden.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence". Wangchuk, a well-known name in India, ended his hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site, India's NDTV website reported. India's state and health ministers had visited Wangchuk in hospital. Reacting to Wangchuk's decision to end his hunger strike, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke wrote in a post on X: "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

offbeat.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
offbeat.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence". Wangchuk, a well-known name in India, ended his hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site, India's NDTV website reported. India's state and health ministers had visited Wangchuk in hospital. Reacting to Wangchuk's decision to end his hunger strike, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke wrote in a post on X: "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.

entertainment.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
entertainment.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

entertainment.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
entertainment.summary: Gruesome images of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez's dismembered body were shown in court on the first day of a preliminary hearing against singer D4vd, whose real name is David Anthony Burke. The evidence, including graphic crime scene photos, links the 21-year-old Burke to the murder and sexual abuse of the teenager, with prosecutors detailing his online purchases of tools associated with the crime. Burke has pleaded not guilty, and the hearing aims to determine if there is enough evidence for the case to proceed to trial.

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
breaking.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on 60 trading partners, accounting for the vast majority of its imports, over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India. They come into effect on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

facts.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
facts.summary: 3 min read Mars Science Laboratory Mission Team Members Jul 23, 2026 Article Written by Lucy Thompson, Senior Research Scientist, University of New Brunswick, Canada Earth planning date: Friday, July 27, 2026 As an APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, I have the privilege of working with the rover engineers most days that I am on operations. The APXS instrument measures the chemistry of rocks, unconsolidated materials and the atmosphere, and is situated on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm. This means that any target of interest that we wish to analyze has to be safe to deploy the arm, APXS, and MAHLI (the closeup imager) to.

facts.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
facts.summary: Extreme heat can take a toll on mental health - and young people and women are among the groups most at risk, according to one study. At the peak of the June heatwave, the warmest June on record in England, all Tavia Watts could think about was how hot it was in her two- and five-year-old's bedroom - above 30C in some cases. "During the heatwave, I've got a high level of anxiety all the time and I'm pretty much useless," she told the BBC. "I've had to leave bedtime when my husband reads [my kids] stories to have a little cry and to calm down, because it's just too hot in their room and I'm afraid for them.

facts.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
facts.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester today, marking a significant step in the government's plan to decentralize power from Westminster. This initiative aims to enhance regional governance, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join the site by the end of next year. The meeting will focus on placing devolution at the forefront of the government's mission to improve economic conditions across the UK.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

politics.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

crisis.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of a large-scale effort to steal technology from top U.S. AI models, specifically claiming it "distilled" capabilities from Anthropic's Fable AI for its K3 model. This allegation follows the unveiling of K3, which has drawn global attention for potentially narrowing the gap between Western and Chinese AI technologies. The U.S. government is investigating Moonshot's access to restricted Nvidia servers used in training its models.

crisis.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
crisis.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A panel of federal health advisers on Thursday narrowly recommended easing access to several peptides popular with wellness influencers and celebrities, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals have not been shown to be safe or effective. In a series of votes, outside experts to the Food and Drug Administration advised the agency to drop current restrictions barring pharmacies from producing injectable peptides with names like BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV. The panel’s vote is not binding but is likely to embolden the health secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr , who has described himself as a “big fan” of peptides and vowed to overturn FDA restrictions put in place under Joe Biden.

health.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
health.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
entities: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.
